title: Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve
tagline: Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve is the first otter conservation reserve in India, located near Hampi in Vijayanagara district in the state of Karnataka.
overview.description: Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve is the first otter conservation reserve in India, located near Hampi in Vijayanagara district in the state of Karnataka. This is a 34 km (21 mi) stretch along Tungabhadra River. The area is well known for smooth-coated otters and soft-shell turtles.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Anegundi
topPlaces.description: Anegundi, previously called Kishkindha, is a village in Gangavathi, Koppal district, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is older than Hampi, situated on the northern bank of the Tungabhadra River.

topPlaces.name: Pampa Sarovar
topPlaces.description: Pampa Sarovara is a lake in Koppal district near Hampi in Karnataka. It is located south of the Tungabhadra River and is considered sacred by Hindus.

topPlaces.name: Hampi (town)
topPlaces.description: Hampi is a city in the Vijayanagara district in the Indian state of Karnataka. Located along the Tungabhadra River in the eastern and central parts of the state, Hampi is near the city of Hospet.

topPlaces.name: Hampi
topPlaces.description: Hampi or Hampe (Kannada: [hɐmpe]), also referred to as the Group of Monuments at Hampi, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in the town of Hampi in Vijayanagara district, east-central Karnataka, India. Hampi predates the Vijayanagara Empire; it is mentioned in the Ramayana and the Puranas of Hinduism as Pampa Devi Tirtha Kshetra.

topPlaces.name: Zenana Mahal
topPlaces.description: The Zenana Mahal (also known as the Zenana Enclosure) is a palace complex located in the city of Hampi, which was the capital of the Vijayanagara Dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Lotus Mahal
topPlaces.description: Lotus Mahal or Chitrangini Mahal is an important secular structure in Hampi, India. The Lotus Mahal is an example of Vijayanagara style of architecture and is a two storied structure built in Rubble masonry and finely plastered.

topPlaces.name: Vijayanagara
topPlaces.description: Vijayanagara (transl. 'City of Victory') is a city located in Vijayanagara district of Karnataka state in India. Vijayanagara was the capital city of the historic Vijayanagara Empire.

topPlaces.name: Virupaksha Temple, Hampi
topPlaces.description: The Virupaksha Temple is located in Hampi in the Vijayanagara district of Karnataka, India, situated on the banks of the river Tungabhadra. Built in the 7th-century, it is dedicated to Virupaksha, a form of Shiva.

items.note: Anegundi, previously called Kishkindha, is a village in Gangavathi, Koppal district, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is older than Hampi, situated on the northern bank of the Tungabhadra River.
Pampa Sarovara is a lake in Koppal district near Hampi in Karnataka. It is located south of the Tungabhadra River and is considered sacred by Hindus.
Hampi is a city in the Vijayanagara district in the Indian state of Karnataka. Located along the Tungabhadra River in the eastern and central parts of the state, Hampi is near the city of Hospet.

Hampi or Hampe (Kannada: [hɐmpe]), also referred to as the Group of Monuments at Hampi, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in the town of Hampi in Vijayanagara district, east-central Karnataka, India. Hampi predates the Vijayanagara Empire; it is mentioned in the Ramayana and the Puranas of Hinduism as Pampa Devi Tirtha Kshetra.
The Zenana Mahal (also known as the Zenana Enclosure) is a palace complex located in the city of Hampi, which was the capital of the Vijayanagara Dynasty.

Lotus Mahal or Chitrangini Mahal is an important secular structure in Hampi, India. The Lotus Mahal is an example of Vijayanagara style of architecture and is a two storied structure built in Rubble masonry and finely plastered.
Vijayanagara (transl. 'City of Victory') is a city located in Vijayanagara district of Karnataka state in India. Vijayanagara was the capital city of the historic Vijayanagara Empire.

The Virupaksha Temple is located in Hampi in the Vijayanagara district of Karnataka, India, situated on the banks of the river Tungabhadra. Built in the 7th-century, it is dedicated to Virupaksha, a form of Shiva.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (147 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tungabhadra Otter Conservation Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Anegundi, Pampa Sarovar, Hampi (town), Hampi, Zenana Mahal.
